protocol.name: weft_shell_unstable_v1

protocol.description:
This protocol defines the boundary between weft-compositor and weft-servo-shell. It allows the shell to manage window slots on behalf of application surfaces that the compositor owns.
The shell binds the weft_shell_manager_v1 global once per connection. The manager creates weft_shell_window_v1 objects, each representing one visual window slot. The compositor is authoritative for geometry, focus, and stacking. The shell is authoritative for window metadata, chrome layout, and the decision to create or destroy a window slot.
Warning: This protocol is unstable and subject to change before version 1 is finalized. Clients must check the version advertised by the compositor and handle unknown events gracefully.

interface.name: zweft_shell_manager_v1
interface.description: Bound once by weft-servo-shell. The manager owns the shell session. If the binding is destroyed the compositor invalidates all window objects that were created through it.

request.name: destroy

description.summary: destroy the manager and end the shell session

request.description: Destroys the manager. All weft_shell_window_v1 objects created through this manager become inert. The compositor will emit window_closed on each before processing the destructor.

request.name: create_window
request.description: Creates a new window slot. The compositor assigns the protocol object identity; no separate window_id is exchanged. The compositor will emit configure on the new object to communicate the effective initial geometry and state.

arg.name: role
arg.summary: window role hint (normal, dialog, panel, overlay)

arg.name: x
arg.summary: requested x position in compositor logical coordinates

arg.name: y
arg.summary: requested y position in compositor logical coordinates

arg.name: width
arg.summary: requested width in compositor logical coordinates

arg.name: height
arg.summary: requested height in compositor logical coordinates

interface.name: zweft_shell_window_v1
interface.description:
Represents one visual window. The compositor controls the effective geometry, stacking, and focus state. The shell controls metadata and may request geometry changes; the compositor may reject or adjust such requests.
A window object becomes inert after window_closed is received. The shell must call destroy on an inert object as soon as possible.

entry.name: fullscreen
entry.summary: window covers the entire output including chrome

entry.name: resizing
entry.summary: an interactive resize is in progress

entry.name: defunct_window
entry.summary: request sent after window_closed was received

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the window object. If the window is still alive the compositor will remove the corresponding window slot from the display. The shell must not send requests on this object after calling destroy.

request.name: update_metadata
request.description: Updates advisory metadata. The compositor may use these values in window decorations or accessibility trees. Updates are not authoritative for policy decisions.

request.name: set_geometry
request.description: Asks the compositor to change the window geometry. The compositor validates the request against output bounds and current policy. The compositor is not required to honor the exact values. A configure event will be sent with the effective geometry that results.

event.name: configure

description.summary: compositor sets effective geometry and state

event.description: Sent by the compositor when the effective geometry or state changes. The shell must treat this as authoritative and update its DOM layout to match. The shell must ack each configure event by sending an update_metadata request or by doing nothing if no metadata changed; no explicit ack request is defined in version 1.

event.name: focus_changed
event.description: Sent when surface-level focus changes for this window slot. The shell updates visual focus state in its DOM but does not override compositor focus policy.

event.name: window_closed

description.summary: window slot is no longer valid

event.description: Sent by the compositor when the window object is being invalidated. After this event the shell must call destroy on the object. Any request sent after window_closed is a protocol error.

event.name: presentation_feedback
event.description: Sent after a frame containing this window's content is presented to the output. Allows the shell to align animations and resize flows with compositor timing. tv_sec and tv_nsec are wall-clock values from the compositor's monotonic clock. refresh is the output refresh interval in nanoseconds; 0 if unknown.
